text: Based on neuropathological findings and neuroimaging from patients suffering from neurodegeneration as well as from evidence derived by transgenic animal models of neurodegeneration, it has been suggested that neurodegeneration is related to neural network dysfunction (Palop et al., 2007b; Seeley et al., 2009). Neurodegeneration leads to impairment of retrograde axonal transport that prohibits the growth factor supply to long-range projection neurons, causing synapse loss, and post-synaptic dendrite retraction that leads to decreases of the neuronal network (Seeley et al., 2009).
